Stag Hunt — Upgrade Status 2026-05-26

Subgoals 0–4 from ~/dev/stag_hunt_goal.md have all been moved forward. The game now has 8 distinct bot policies, 5 prey kinds with differentiated mechanics (including elephant trample and the new moose gut/shove), a JSONL event log + replay-friendly eval harness, a hysteretic energy-aware retreat loop in every bot, and a working sprite-protocol packet for self-energy.

Holes that are honest: elephant coordination is still fragile, stag captures are noisy across seeds, and the modeler hasn't been re-evaluated post-energy-fix. Reward balance is "first guess, second guess" not "playtested." Detail below.

Replay — 8 bots, 2 rounds × 5 min

One of each policy, slots 0–7, seed 5744151. Recorded from tmp/eval/showcase-8bot-5min/events.jsonl. Every player and prey tile-change is logged (player_move / prey_move), so the replay is grid-exact — no interpolation. Catches/tramples/guts flash in real time.

round 1 · t=0 / 2160

Color → Policy

TL;DR — what's solid, what's open

Solid

  • All 8 bots build, connect, parse the new 0x08 energy packet, and follow their stated strategy.
  • Energy regression that froze bots at 0–1 in long games is fully closed (rest-when-low, hysteresis 30 → 60).
  • Moose feels like an actual moose (gut+shove at cardinal adjacency, almost-safe diagonals).
  • Per-prey flee tuning gives each kind a distinct feel.
  • Replay-friendly: every catch, trample, moose_gut, round_start, etc. is in events.jsonl; the eval harness round-trips it.

Open

  • 4-bot elephant captures stay around 1/round in focus mode and approach 0 on the full 32×32 map — the synchronized dash needs a stricter "all 4 ready" gate or a longer elephant cadence.
  • Stag captures are noisy seed-to-seed (0–3/game).
  • Reward balance is calibrated by intuition, not playtest. Rabbit energy was just dropped 25 → 15 — 17 or 20 may be the real sweet spot.
  • No persistent modeler memory across rounds (intentionally).
  • No in-page replay player yet… except for the one right above.

Players

Stated strategy comes from each bot's own docstrings. Performance numbers come from the 60s self-play / mixed-play audits recorded in stag_hunt/stats.md. Most recent audit: 2026-05-23, post energy-awareness.

rabbiteer working
Find the nearest rabbit. Step onto it. That's the whole policy.
What I fixedWas already working at baseline; added 0x08 packet parse, then energy-rest gate (don't keep moving below 30 energy).
Self-play perf36 rabbits / 60s with 4× rabbiteer. Zero false targets.
Mixed perf13 rabbits in 75s as part of a 6-bot roster — top scorer of that game.
Why it still mattersAnchors the "low cooperation" end of the bot spectrum. The modeler / big_game_hunter both have to outperform a roomful of rabbiteers to be interesting.
stag_hunter working
Pick a stag, claim the opposing side from any ally that's already adjacent, encircle with a partner. Skip stags that already have 2+ allies adjacent (they're someone else's catch).
Original behaviourWandered. 0 stags / 60s at baseline (commit 5d99da8).
Key fixRank-based side assignment + cost function that penalises stags allies are closer to than us. Plus navigateAvoiding so the BFS routes around ally tiles instead of bouncing.
2× self-play6 stags + 2 rabbits in 60s (commit 9c73f07 audit).
4× self-play3 stags but uneven distribution between bots — hint for future stable-matching work.
Recent fixRest-when-low so it stops freezing at energy 0 in long games (subject of the round-2 user feedback).
moose_hunter working
Moose needs 3 hunters on cardinal sides simultaneously. Use the min-max-hunter-distance picker so all hunters converge on the same target. Approach diagonally (almost-safe), then step onto a cardinal side when others are in position.
Original behaviour0–1 moose/game, very flaky (4d5789a). Hunters split between targets.
Key fix #1Min-max-hunter-distance target picker → all hunters converge on the same moose.
Key fix #2Rank-based side claim + navigateAvoiding for ally collision-free pathing.
3× self-play4–5 moose / 60s consistently.
4× self-play after moose-gut + energy rest5 moose + 4 rabbit + 1 stag across 3×75s rounds. Energy stays in the 25–50 range — bots are no longer fully wrecked.
OpenNow retreats from moose if it's cardinal-1 and energy < 30, since gut can hit. Has not yet been tuned for the new diagonal-approach pattern.
elephant_hunter partial
Elephants need all 4 cardinal sides. Rank determines which corner (chebyshev-2 diagonal — safe from cardinal trample) each hunter heads to; once 4 hunters are in chebyshev 2 of the same elephant, they all dash to their cardinal side on the same tick.
Original behaviour0–1 elephant per game (4d5789a). Elephants ran away before encirclement.
Server sideMade elephants move cardinal-only with 1-tile-or-stride cadence, trample-and-stop instead of pass-through. Trample = -30 energy + red glow, plus a 4-frame pixel-level slide animation so it reads as a leap, not a teleport.
Bot sideCorner pre-positioning + synchronized dash + min-max-hunter-distance picker so all hunters converge.
Then a regressionLong games starved bots to 0 energy. Added 0x08 packet (server publishes self-energy) and a hysteretic retreat (energy < 32 → run to chebyshev 5; energy ≥ 64 → rejoin). Commit exception: if already at chebyshev 2 with 2+ allies near, don't bail.
Focus-eval perf1.0–1.2 captures/round across seeds, 4 bots, elephants spawning near the player centroid. End-of-round energies now 22–180 instead of 0–1.
Full-map perfStill about 0 captures on 32×32 mixed prey — elephants spawn far away, hunters never converge in time.
Honest gapThe 4-of-4 synchronized commit is the bottleneck. Suggestions in "Open Questions" below.
big_game_hunter working
Count nearby allies → derive which prey kinds are catchable at that coalition size → pick the highest-reward catchable prey in PreferredReachChebyshev, else the nearest catchable.
Self-play perf4× big_game_hunter over 60s: 11 rabbits + 1 boar + 2 stags. Catches across the size ladder as the coalition shifts. (Renamed from coordinator on 2026-05-26 — the name was misleading; this bot doesn't coordinate with anyone, it just picks the biggest catchable prey for its current coalition size.)
Recent fixEnergy-rest gate (writes resting (energy N) to its intent string so it shows up in --debug-intent output).
sidekick working
Maintain a priority list of nearby players, follow the highest-priority one, jump in to assist whenever they're adjacent to a 2+-player prey. Favor bigger game in a tie (preyRank: Elephant 5 → Rabbit 1).
Mixed perf (1× sidekick + 2× stag_hunter)Sidekick caught 2 stags + 0 rabbits in 60s, validating it skips solo-catch prey.
Recent fixEnergy-rest gate.
nearest_hunter working
Take the nearest reachable prey at the current coalition size.
Self-play perf4× nearest_hunter in 60s: 14 rabbits + 3 boars + 5 stags. Mix-of-everything as designed.
Recent fixEnergy-rest gate.
modeler untested post-energy-fix
Track per-color memory: count seenCatch + attempts + failures per prey kind. Score hunt targets as reward × P(cooperation given this ally roster) × 1/dist.
Pre-regression perfmodeler + 3 rabbiteer mix: modeler was top scorer at 13 rabbits + 2 stags. It correctly drops elephant attempts when allies don't follow.
Current stateHas the 0x08 parser and energy-rest gate. Has not been re-evaluated against a full bot mix since the energy fix landed; should still be ~good because the rest gate doesn't affect modeling, but worth confirming.

Prey / mechanics

Rabbit

Single-player catch. Flees fast (75/50/25% by distance). Was the dominant XP source; rabbit energy reward just dropped 25 → 15 to make rabbit-spamming a slightly worse fuel strategy. Guess: 17 or 20 might be the real number; needs another playtest pass. Score reward stays at 1.

Boar

2-player catch. Designed as a fuel prey (90 energy / 3 score). Already balanced as "more food than fame." Not modified this pass.

Stag

2-player coop. Flees moderately (52/35/17%). Reward 60 energy / 5 score. Stag captures stay noisy seed-to-seed (0–3/game), driven mostly by spawn luck — both hunters need to see the same stag before either commits. Not modified this pass.

Moose redesigned this pass

3-player coop on cardinal sides. Reward 140 energy / 10 score. New "gut" behavior:

Cardinal-adjacent (N/S/E/W of moose)30% chance per think tick to gut — -10 energy, red trample glow, player shoved 1 tile further from the moose if open. Animation: moose slides halfway forward toward the player's tile, then bounces back; player simultaneously slides one tile in the push direction.
Diagonal-adjacent5% chance — "almost safe" so hunters can pre-position diagonally before committing to a cardinal side.
Flee at chebyshev 115% (was 30%) — mostly guts instead.
Flee at chebyshev 230% (was 20%) — most jumpy at "spotted" range.
Flee at chebyshev 3+10% (unchanged).
EventNew JSONL event moose_gut with pushed bool.

Elephant redesigned earlier this branch

MovementCardinal-only, 12–24 tick cooldown (flat — earlier "scale with nearby hunters" version felt vicious, reverted).
Stride30% chance after a clean step to chain 1–3 more steps in the same direction (a "charge").
Trample-30 energy, red glow, 4-frame pixel slide where the elephant leaps over the trampled player to the far tile. If the far tile is blocked, the elephant stays put — that's the loophole that lets a fully-encircled elephant get captured.
Reward220 energy / 18 score.

Server / engine changes

  • Sprite packet 0x08 (energy) emitted in every per-player frame; viewers and bots all updated (the JS in player_client.html + global_client.html originally threw on unknown packet types and disconnected — fixed).
  • Per-kind flee probability + per-kind think interval (bigger animals react slower).
  • Elephant trample-and-stop with pixel-level slide animation; moose gut with player slide animation.
  • JSONL event log via --event-log:PATH (catch, trample, moose_gut, prey_spawn, snapshot, round_start, round_end, server_start, player_connect, tournament_end).
  • focus mode ("focus": "elephant" in config) for fast catch-dynamics testing — spawns only elephants, near the player centroid, cap 1 at a time. focus_eval.sh wraps it.
  • Reset between rounds properly clears killGlow, trampleGlow, pushStep, rechargeCounter.

Energy / balance

Current numbers:

ConstantValueNotes
StartEnergy120Each round starts here.
MaxEnergy200Hard cap; only reachable by hunting big prey.
PassiveRechargeMax100Resting only refills to here.
PassiveRechargeInterval18~0.75s for +1 at 24 fps.
MoveEnergyCost2Per successful step.
PlayerMoveCooldownTicks5~5 moves/sec; net drain ~8.6/s when moving constantly.
RabbitEnergyReward15 ↓ from 25Guess: 17 or 20 may be better.
BoarEnergyReward90The fuel prey.
StagEnergyReward60
MooseEnergyReward140
ElephantEnergyReward220
ElephantTrampleEnergyLoss30
MooseGutEnergyLoss10Less brutal than trample but still meaningful.

Score table: Rabbit 1, Boar 3, Stag 5, Moose 10, Elephant 18. (A ×5/3 bump to lift coop prey was tried on 2026-05-26 and reverted same day — pathfinding works but coop catches aren't yet reliable enough to justify paying them more.)

Latest balance eval (2026-05-24)

3 rounds × 75s = 225s of game time per scenario. Seed 5744151.

4× moose_hunter (self-play)

  • Catches: 5 moose, 4 rabbit, 1 stag
  • moose_gut events: 45
  • Tramples: 2 (from incidental elephants)
  • End-round energies: 25–50 (healthy)
  • Per-round scores: r1=[30,0,30,30] · r2=[0,0,0,0] · r3=[21,25,21,7]

2× stag + 2× nearest + 2× rabbiteer

  • Catches: 71 rabbit, 2 stag, 2 boar
  • End-round energies: 33–77 across all bots
  • Top scorer: rabbiteer at 13. Stags scored 2–7. Rabbit-spam still beats stag coop here.

4× elephant_hunter (self-play, full map)

  • Catches: 4 rabbit, 1 stag, 0 elephants across 3 rounds
  • Tramples: 2 (down from previous 162-in-one-session disaster)
  • End-round energies: 7–62 (one bot got close to depletion once)
  • Elephants spawn far on full 32×32 map; hunters never converge in time.

Replay / live viewing

Beyond the canvas player above, three other ways to look at games:

  1. Live: any active eval leaves the server on http://localhost:8090/global (spectator) and http://localhost:8090/player (slot 7 stays open when 7 bots are launched on slots 0–6).
  2. Event log: tmp/eval/<timestamp>/events.jsonl — one JSON line per event with tick + slot + name + position + kind. Grep-friendly.
  3. Per-bot debug: tmp/eval/<timestamp>/bots/<tag>.log for connect/disconnect + bot stdout.

Open questions for the game itself

  • Is the rabbiteer-vs-coop-hunter scoring honest? In the mixed 6-bot run, rabbiteers (13 score) beat stag_hunters (2–7 score) and nearest_hunters (8). That's the right direction (you have to actually coordinate to outscore rabbit-spam) but it's narrow. Stag reward at 5 may need to be 6–7; or rabbit reward at 1 could become 0 with food-only payoff. Worth a deliberate balance pass.
  • Elephant convergence on the full map. The synchronized-dash works when hunters all see the same elephant. On a 32×32 map with random elephant spawns, that pre-condition is rare. Options: spawn elephants near the player cluster as a soft rule, slow the elephant cadence further (12 → 24+ ticks min), or add a "rally point" gesture so one hunter can call others over.
  • Stag coordination is brittle. 2-player coop needs both hunters to see the stag before either commits. Maybe stag should be slightly easier to corner (e.g. it tries to back into a corner once trapped) so 2v1 reliably wins.
  • Should boar be approachable solo? 2-player coop on a low-score prey feels niche. Consider making it 1-player but slow you down (something like a poison/grass-burn tick).
  • Map design. Currently random 32×32 with scattered trees/rocks. No chokepoints, no "moose corridor," no "rabbit field." Some level design might help the bots' policies be legible to a human watching.
  • Energy ceiling vs. recharge ceiling. Resting only refills to 100; starting energy is 120. So unless you eat big game, you can never recover the buffer you start with. This is intentional ("you need to hunt to thrive") but a fresh player would probably find it surprising. Maybe recharge to 120, with bigger overflow up to 200 only from kills.

Open work for the bots (less important per goal doc)

  • Stable matching between hunters and big game (one stag = a specific pair, one moose = a specific trio, deterministically).
  • Persistent modeler memory across rounds so it can learn faster in tournament play.
  • Extract a shared bot framework — five files share ~500 lines of identical sprite/camera/nav boilerplate.
  • Modeler re-audit after the energy/rest gate (likely fine, not confirmed).

Subgoals checklist

#SubgoalState
0Replay / observability infraJSONL event log + eval.sh + focus_eval.sh + in-page replay
1Existing players actually follow their stated strategiesAudited; all 5 pass
2moose_hunter + elephant_hunterBuilt. moose_hunter solid. elephant_hunter works in focus mode, partial on full map.
3modeler tracks per-color capabilityBuilt; outscored rabbiteer mix pre-regression. Needs post-energy-fix audit.
4Balance points/energyFirst-pass: per-kind flee, big-game rewards, rabbit fuel reduction. Real playtest pass not done.

Source: ~/dev/bitworld/stag_hunt/. Branch: stag-hunt-upgrade. Status notes also in stag_hunt/learnings.md and stag_hunt/stats.md; commit history shows iteration order.
